mission.title: Install and Configure Fluentd on Kubernetes
mission.description: Fluentd is an open-source log collector that helps unify your logging infrastructure by collecting events from various data sources and writing them to multiple destinations. Installing Fluentd allows you to manage and streamline your logging processes effectively.

steps.title: Add the Fluentd Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Fluentd Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration. This allows you to install Fluentd using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Fluentd using Helm
steps.description:
Install Fluentd in a new namespace called 'fluentd' with the specified version.
```bash
helm install fluentd fluent/fluentd --version 1.19.2 --namespace fluentd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Fluentd pods are running successfully in the 'fluentd' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fluentd
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Fluentd deployment to ensure it runs efficiently. Edit the values.yaml file or use the following command to set limits directly:
```bash
kubectl patch deployment fluentd -n fluentd --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Fluentd logs
steps.description:
Use port-forwarding to access Fluentd logs locally. This allows you to check the logs directly from your machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/fluentd -n fluentd 24224:24224
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Fluentd will have the Fluentd pods running in the 'fluentd' namespace, and you will be able to access the logs via port-forwarding. You should also see the resource limits applied to the Fluentd deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Fluentd release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources, but not the CRDs or persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall fluentd --namespace fluentd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Fluentd to ensure complete cleanup.
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace fluentd
kubectl delete crd fluentd.*
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'fluentd' namespace to remove all remaining resources and verify that it has been deleted.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace fluentd
kubectl get namespaces | grep fluentd
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current Fluentd configuration and any relevant data. You can use the following command to export the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -o yaml > fluentd-backup-configmap.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Fluentd to the desired version. Replace '1.19.3' with the new version you want to upgrade to.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade fluentd fluent/fluentd --version 1.19.3 --namespace fluentd
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Fluentd pods are running the new version successfully in the 'fluentd' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fluentd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Fluentd pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. This can indicate misconfiguration or resource issues.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace fluentd
```

troubleshooting.title: Fluentd service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Fluentd service, ensure that the service is running and check the port-forwarding command. Verify the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace fluentd
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If Fluentd is consuming too much memory, consider adjusting the resource limits. Check the current resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace fluentd
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If changes to the Fluentd configuration are not reflected, ensure that the correct ConfigMap is being used and restart the pods to apply changes:
```bash
kubectl rollout restart deployment/fluentd --namespace fluentd
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
